actual_values.regime_actual: B-dominated -- Uranus > Neptune at actual F_heat

scientific_note: Gap 7 (Twin Paradox): BCM correctly predicts m=2 for both ice giants. J_ind amplitude ordering (Uranus > Neptune) persists across all realistic F_heat values because the 33% stronger B-field of Uranus cannot be overcome by convective velocity alone at ionic ocean conductivities. The model operates in a B-dominated regime for both planets. Resolution requires direct measurement of convective flow velocities at dynamo depth -- a target for the Uranus Orbiter and Probe mission.
